records.ety: [Pref. in- not + fallible: cf. F. infallible.]

defs: Not fallible; not capable of erring; entirely exempt from liability to mistake; unerring; inerrable.
Not liable to fail, deceive, or disappoint; indubitable; sure; certain; as, infallible evidence; infallible success; an infallible remedy.
quotes: To whom also he showed himself alive, after his passion, by many infallible proofs.

defs: Incapable of error in defining doctrines touching faith or morals. See Papal infallibility, under Infallibility.
